specification.topic-statement: The fork argument parsing system extracts an optional model override and an optional system prompt from the raw argument string of the fork command, and applies or inherits provider configuration for the child agent.

scope.in-scope: Tokenization of fork command arguments, extraction of the model specification flag and the quoted prompt string, validation of argument syntax, application of a model override to a child agent, and inheritance of provider configuration from a parent agent.
scope.boundary: How the child agent is created, how its conversation history is initialized, and how it is integrated into the REPL are handled by the agent fork subsystem. This topic covers only the argument parsing phase and the resulting configuration changes to the child agent record.
How model names are parsed into provider and thinking-level components is delegated to the model selection subsystem.

field.name: model specification
field.note: value following the --model flag; absent if flag not present

field.name: system prompt
field.note: content extracted from between double quotes; absent if no quoted string present

behavior.argument-parsing:
The raw argument string is tokenized to extract two optional components: a model specification and a quoted system prompt. The two components may appear in either order.
The model specification is indicated by the flag "--model" followed by a whitespace-delimited token. If "--model" appears without a following token, a syntax error is returned.
The system prompt is enclosed in double quotes. The content between the opening and closing quote is extracted verbatim. If an opening quote is present without a matching closing quote, a syntax error is returned. An empty quoted string (two adjacent double-quote characters) is valid and produces an empty prompt string.
Any non-whitespace text that is neither part of the "--model" flag, its value, nor a quoted string is treated as a syntax error.
The "--model" flag and the quoted string may appear in any order relative to each other. "/fork --model gpt-x \"prompt\"" and "/fork \"prompt\" --model gpt-x" are equivalent.
An absent or whitespace-only argument string is accepted without error. Both the model specification and the system prompt are treated as absent.
behavior.model-override-application:
When a model specification is present in the parsed arguments, it is applied to the child agent. The specification is parsed by the model selection subsystem to extract a provider identifier, a model name, and an optional thinking level.
If the model name is not recognized, an error is returned. If a thinking level is specified, it is applied to the child agent. If no thinking level is specified, the child agent's thinking level is not modified at this stage.
The child agent's provider and model fields are updated to the resolved values.
The model specification string may optionally include a thinking level in the form "model/level". If the level component is absent, the child agent's existing thinking level is preserved.
behavior.provider-config-inheritance:
When no model specification is present in the parsed arguments, the child agent inherits the parent agent's provider configuration. The parent's provider identifier, model name, and thinking level are copied to the child agent.
If either the parent's provider or model field is absent (null), that field is not copied.
A parent agent that has not yet made an LLM request may have null provider and model fields. Inheritance skips null fields silently, leaving the child with no provider or model configured.
